purpose: Owns the yolo-jail configuration lifecycle on the host: JSON5/JSONC parsing and hierarchical merging (user config → workspace yolo-jail.jsonc → auto-merged yolo-jail.local.jsonc → include_if_found chains), the full schema validation with human-readable error strings, env_sources dotenv resolution, the config-change safety snapshot/diff/approve flow, the `yolo check` doctor (runtime/nix/GPU/KVM/image/loophole/socket/disk probes), `yolo init`/`init-user-config` scaffolding, the `yolo config-ref` reference text, and generation of per-jail AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md briefings plus per-agent skills staging dirs that get bind-mounted into containers.

files.role: JSONC load/merge/validate core: _load_jsonc_file (pyjson5), merge_config, _load_jsonc_with_includes, load_workspace_config, load_config, schema constants (KNOWN_*_KEYS, regexes), _validate_config (+helpers), _parse_dotenv/_resolve_env_sources, _config_snapshot_path/_check_config_changes, derived helpers (_effective_packages, _normalize_blocked_tools, _merge_mise_tools/_merge_mise_disabled_tools, selected_agents, _effective_mcp_server_names, _filter_mcp_servers_by_env), ConfigError

yolo check (2105 lines): environment/doctor probes — container runtime liveness, nix daemon + trust, macOS platform, global storage, strict config parse + merged validation, entrypoint dry-run, GPU (NVIDIA CDI / AMD ROCm device nodes), KVM, nix image build preflight (_nix_dry_run_will_build, _has_linux_builder, _preflight_builder_needs, _diagnose_nix_build_failure), image loaded/running-jails/orphan cleanup, loophole self-checks + broker creds freshness + per-jail relay socket probes, disk usage, inline-loophole exec checks; PASS/FAIL/WARN badge output; exit 1 on any fail
yolo config-ref: one giant rich-markup string documenting every config field, merge rules, env vars, commands, in-jail layout. Pure static output

yolo init (writes commented yolo-jail.jsonc template, honors repeatable --mount/-m, appends .yolo/ to .gitignore, prints agent-briefing block) and yolo init-user-config (writes ~/.config/yolo-jail/config.jsonc template)

generate_agents_md (per-agent briefing files in AGENTS_DIR/<cname>/: jail-managed markdown + agents_md_extra + host briefing prepended; provisioning-failure breadcrumb from .yolo/startup.log; yolo-source-tree detection via pyproject.toml), _write_briefing (hardlink-safe inode-preserving truncate), _prepare_skills/_copy_skill_subdirs (per-agent skills-<agent>/ staging with built-in jail-startup SKILL.md; clears dir CONTENTS only to keep the bind-mounted inode)

public_surface.name: yolo check
public_surface.detail: Flags: --build/--no-build (default on; --no-build skips nix build preflight). Sections printed in fixed order: Container Runtime, Nix, (macOS Platform), Global Storage, Config Files, Merged Configuration, Entrypoint Dry-Run, (macos-user backend), (GPU NVIDIA/AMD), (KVM), Image Build, Container Image, Running Jails (interactive 'Stop N orphaned jail(s)? [y/N]' prompt with `<runtime> rm -f`), Loopholes, Per-jail host-service liveness, Disk usage, Loopholes — inline daemons, Summary. Badges are literal text '[PASS]'/'[FAIL]'/'[WARN]' (rich-colored, 6 cols); multi-line notes indented with '       -> ' first line then 10 spaces. Exit 1 if any FAIL (early-exits after Config Files or Merged Configuration failures), else 0.
public_surface.consumers: humans + agents after every config edit; run_cmd.py:3144 invokes check; docs mandate it

public_surface.name: yolo init
public_surface.detail:
--mount/-m repeatable ('HOST' or 'HOST:/ctx/NAME'). Writes commented yolo-jail.jsonc template to cwd (baking mounts array if -m given); no-op + briefing if file exists ('yolo-jail.jsonc already exists.'). Appends '
# YOLO Jail workspace state
.yolo/
' to .gitignore if '.yolo/' absent (creates .gitignore if missing). Prints 'Created yolo-jail.jsonc' then a long AGENT BRIEFING block (handover.md mandate, jail-startup skill hint).

public_surface.name: yolo init-user-config
public_surface.detail: Creates ~/.config/yolo-jail/config.jsonc (mkdir -p parent) from a commented template; prints 'Created <path>' or '<path> already exists.'

public_surface.name: yolo config-ref
public_surface.detail: Prints the full static configuration reference (rich markup). Agents parse it as documentation; AGENTS.md and shims point at it.

public_surface.name: yolo-jail.jsonc / yolo-jail.local.jsonc / ~/.config/yolo-jail/config.jsonc
public_surface.detail: JSON5 (pyjson5): comments, trailing commas, single quotes, unquoted keys all accepted. Must be a top-level object (else strict ConfigError 'X must contain a top-level JSON object' / non-strict stderr Warning + {}). Merge order: user → workspace → workspace local (auto-merged, shared include-cycle seen-set so an explicit include of the local file doesn't double-merge). include_if_found: list of RELATIVE paths (absolute/'~' rejected), resolved against the including file's dir, missing silently skipped, recursive with cycle detection (resolved-Path seen set), key consumed by loader (never in merged config).
public_surface.consumers: whole CLI via load_config; entrypoint indirectly via env vars run_cmd derives

public_surface.name: merge semantics
public_surface.detail: merge_config: dicts deep-merge (override wins per key); lists UNION-dedup preserving base order, dedup key = json.dumps(item, sort_keys=True, default=str) — EXCEPT 'agents' which is replace-not-union (_OVERRIDE_LIST_KEYS); scalars/mismatched types: override replaces. Parity-critical: canonical-JSON dedup equality for dict list items.

public_surface.name: schema (KNOWN_TOP_LEVEL_CONFIG_KEYS)
public_surface.detail: runtime ('podman'|'container'|'macos-user'; 'docker' → dedicated removal error), repo_path (str, user-config only — workspace use warns), agents (list from VALID_AGENTS: claude,copilot,gemini,opencode,pi,codex; default ['claude']; empty list warns), packages (str matching ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+(\.[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)?$ or object {name,nixpkgs|version+url+hash|outputs} — nixpkgs XOR version-override; dotted name string-only), mounts ('host' or 'host:/abs' — nonexistent host path warns), workspace_readonly / per_side_paths (relative, no '..'; per_side rejects ''/'.'), network {mode bridge|host, ports ['h:c'|'ip:h:c' opt '/tcp|udp', 1-65535], forward_host_ports [int|'l:h']} (host mode + ports → warn ignored), security.blocked_tools (str | {name,message,suggestion,block_flags}; default ['grep','find'] with built-in messages/block_flags; dict form merges defaults, user wins), mise_tools {name:version} (default {'neovim':'stable'}), lsp_servers {name:{command,args,fileExtensions(required)}}, mcp_presets (chrome-devtools|sequential-thinking), mcp_servers {name: null|{command,args,env(str:str),requires_env([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)}}, devices (str path — missing warns — or {usb 'hhhh:hhhh' XOR cgroup_rule, description}), gpu {enabled,vendor nvidia|amd,devices str,mode(amd: devices|cdi),capabilities(nvidia only: compute,utility,graphics,video,display,compat32),hsa_override_gfx_version(amd),seccomp_unconfined(amd),vaapi(amd, implies mesa+libva-utils packages)}, resources {memory ^\d+[bkmgBKMG]?$, cpus positive number/numeric str, pids_limit positive int}, env_sources (ordered list: str path | inline {KEY:val}, key regex enforced; legacy 'env' key → removal error), host_claude_files (filenames only, no '/'), loopholes {name ^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_-]{0,63}$; 'cgroup-delegate' reserved; override-of-known-loophole shape = enabled/env/jail_env only ('command' → dedicated error; 'env' without host_daemon → error); override-shaped-but-unknown → validate + warn no-op; else inline service {command non-empty str list required, env str:str, jail_socket must start '/run/yolo-services/'}}, host_processes {visible,fields str lists}, journal (bool or 'off'|'user'|'full'), kvm (bool), prune (warn_threshold_gb consumed by check), ephemeral_storage ('volume'|'tmpfs'), include_if_found, agents_md_extra (str). Error style: 'config.<dotted.path>[idx]: <message>' — exact strings are parity surface (tests assert substrings). Same-file mcp_presets+null conflict checked per-file, not on merged config.

public_surface.name: <workspace>/.yolo/config-snapshot.json
public_surface.detail: Normalized merged config: json.dumps(config, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + '\n'. First run: written silently, proceed. Unchanged (compared after rstrip of old): proceed. Changed: colored unified diff (difflib.unified_diff, fromfile='previous config', tofile='current config') + prompt 'Accept these config changes? [y/N] ' (y/yes accepts+rewrites; anything else/EOF/SIGINT aborts, returns False → jail not started). Non-tty stdin: auto-accept with yellow warning. Go must write byte-identical normalization or every port swap triggers a spurious approval diff.

public_surface.name: AGENTS_DIR/<cname>/ briefing files
public_surface.detail:
~/.local/share/yolo-jail/agents/<cname>/{CLAUDE.md, AGENTS-copilot.md, AGENTS-gemini.md, ...} (staging name per agent spec). Content = optional host briefing (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md etc.) + '
---

' + jail-managed markdown ('# YOLO Jail Environment' with workspace path, network line, forwarded ports, resource limits, loopholes list, blocked tools, context mounts, Limitations, Packages & Resource Limits, Skills, optional 'Testing Changes to yolo-jail' when workspace is the yolo-jail source tree, optional agents_md_extra appended). Provisioning-failure breadcrumb inserted when .yolo/startup.log contains 'PROVISIONING FAILED'. Write must preserve inode when nlink==1 (in-place truncate keeps running jails' bind mounts live) but unlink first when nlink>1 (hardlink-dedup fusion hazard).
public_surface.consumers: run_cmd mounts these :ro over ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md etc.; refreshed on every yolo invocation

public_surface.name: AGENTS_DIR/<cname>/skills-<agent>/
public_surface.detail: Per-agent skills staging: built-in jail-startup/SKILL.md (verbatim constant) + 1:1 copy of ~/.<agent>/skills/ subdirs (copytree, symlinks followed). MUST clear contents inside the dir, never rmtree the dir itself (bind mounts pin the inode). Mounted :ro at /home/agent/.<agent>/skills.

public_surface.name: YOLO_VERSION
public_surface.detail: Read as in-jail sentinel: presence skips host-only checks (disk usage, loopholes, image, amdgpu/kvm/device nodes, inline-loophole exec checks, macos-user probes, host-service liveness).

public_surface.name: YOLO_RUNTIME
public_surface.detail: Read in check for early runtime pick and offline-runtime grading (only warns about a non-live runtime if explicitly selected). Overrides config 'runtime'.

public_surface.name: YOLO_EXTRA_PACKAGES
public_surface.detail: SET (json.dumps of effective packages list) in the env of the `nix build .#ociImage --impure --dry-run` subprocess; same contract as the real build in image.py.

public_surface.name: yolo check exit status
public_surface.detail: typer.Exit(1) when failed>0 — raised early after Config Files parse errors, after Merged Configuration errors, or at the end; 0 otherwise. Warnings never affect exit code.

public_surface.name: env_sources dotenv
public_surface.detail: _parse_dotenv: '#' comments and blanks skipped; 'export ' prefix stripped; no '=' → skip; key must match ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ else skip; value stripped then matching outer single/double quotes removed (only if len>=2 and first==last). _resolve_env_sources: entries in order, inline dicts apply directly, later wins; string paths ~-expanded, relative resolved against workspace; missing/unreadable file → yellow console warning + skip (never fatal).

public_surface.name: check-time unix socket probes
public_surface.detail: Per running jail, per external-daemon loophole: connect(AF_UNIX SOCK_STREAM, timeout 2.0) to <sockets_dir>/<name>.sock — accept=PASS, refuse=FAIL. Broker relay gets 4-layer diagnosis: socket-file-absent | connect-refused | connected-but-broker-ping-fails (_broker_ping) | host-pong-but-invisible-in-jail (via `<runtime> exec <cname> sh -c 'test -S <jail_sock>'`, rc0=visible, rc1=absent, else inconclusive).

public_surface.name: shared creds freshness read
public_surface.detail: check reads ~/.local/share/yolo-jail/home/.claude-shared-credentials/.credentials.json → int(data['claudeAiOauth']['expiresAt']) ms; absent/zero-byte = pre-login (silent); unreadable = warn; expired = fail; <1h = warn; formats durations as 'Nm'/'NhMm'; mtime age reported as 'last write X ago'.

public_surface.name: nix build dry-run stderr parsing
public_surface.detail: _WILL_BUILD_RE = ^(this derivation|these \d+ derivations) will be built: (MULTILINE) against `nix --extra-experimental-features 'nix-command flakes' build .#ociImage --impure --dry-run` STDERR; collects trailing *.drv basenames. None (inconclusive) on subprocess error or nonzero-exit-without-plan — callers must never treat None as a cache miss. _has_linux_builder parses `nix config show` 'builders =' line incl. '@/etc/nix/machines' files, requires exact 'aarch64-linux' in systems field with max_jobs != '0'.

internal_deps: src/cli/paths.py (USER_CONFIG_PATH, GLOBAL_STORAGE/HOME/MISE, AGENTS_DIR, CONTAINER_DIR, BUILD_DIR, ALL_RUNTIMES/NATIVE_RUNTIMES/SUPPORTED_RUNTIMES, IS_MACOS, JAIL_HOST_SERVICES_DIR, BUILTIN_CGROUP_LOOPHOLE_NAME)
src/entrypoint/agent_registry.py (DEFAULT_AGENTS, VALID_AGENTS, resolve_agents — dual-identity import, shared with the in-image entrypoint package)

third_party_deps.used_for: parsing all config files (full JSON5: comments, trailing commas, single quotes, unquoted keys, hex numbers)
third_party_deps.go_equivalent: github.com/titanous/json5 or yosuke-furukawa/json5 port; MUST fuzz-compare acceptance against pyjson5 — leniency differences are a silent config-drop hazard

third_party_deps.used_for: command registration, --build/--no-build bool flag, repeatable --mount, typer.echo, typer.Exit(1)
third_party_deps.go_equivalent: cobra/urfave-cli; replicate exit code + stderr Warning: prefix

third_party_deps.used_for: all check/init/config-ref output markup, colored diff, console.input prompt
third_party_deps.go_equivalent: manual ANSI or lipgloss; parity should be tested on NO_COLOR/plain text — badge text '[PASS]'/'[FAIL]'/'[WARN]' and indentation are the stable surface

third_party_deps.used_for: unified_diff of config snapshot with 'previous config'/'current config' headers
third_party_deps.go_equivalent: port of Myers unified diff (e.g. pkg/diff); hunk headers need not be byte-identical for correctness but should be for golden tests

side_effects: creates/clears/writes AGENTS_DIR/<cname>/ briefings + skills-<agent>/ (rmtree of dir CONTENTS, copytree from ~/.<agent>/skills/)
reads <workspace>/.yolo/startup.log, ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md & per-agent host briefings, host skills dirs

check: ensure_global_storage() (creates ~/.local/share/yolo-jail tree), runs nix build (creates+unlinks BUILD_DIR/check-result out-link), execs podman/container/nix/nvidia-smi/rocminfo/mount/runc-which/sandbox-exec probes, connects unix sockets, reads /etc/cdi/*.yaml|json, /sys/module/amdgpu, /dev/kfd, /dev/dri/renderD*, /dev/kvm, /etc/nix/*
check interactive: may `<runtime> rm -f <cname>` orphaned jails + cleanup_container_tracking after y/N prompt
prompts on stdin (config-change approval; orphan cleanup); auto-accepts config changes when stdin is not a tty
python_specifics: pyjson5 leniency defines the accepted config dialect — no Go lib is guaranteed bit-compatible; needs a corpus differential test

list dedup identity via json.dumps(item, sort_keys=True, default=str) — Go needs canonical-JSON marshaling with recursive key sort AND a default=str fallback for non-JSON types (rare in practice since input came from JSON5, but numbers: Python renders 4 vs 4.0 distinctly; pyjson5 may parse 4 as int, 4.0 as float)

snapshot normalization json.dumps(indent=2, sort_keys=True): int/float repr, unicode escaping (ensure_ascii=True default → non-ASCII escaped as \uXXXX), key sort — must match byte-for-byte or the y/N approval prompt fires spuriously on Python↔Go swap

dual-identity import (src.entrypoint vs entrypoint) and lazy circular imports (from . import _resolve_repo_root inside check(), from .config inside macos_user) — Go package layout must break the cycle structurally
sys.stdin.isatty() gate + input() with EOFError/KeyboardInterrupt → abort; rich console.input for orphan prompt
closures ok/warn/fail mutating nonlocal counters thread the whole 1000-line check() body — Go port wants a reporter struct
rich markup embedded in nearly every user-facing string incl. config_ref's 660-line literal; rf-strings escaping '\[PASS]' so rich doesn't eat the bracket
os.access(node, R_OK|W_OK), grp.getgrgid, os.getgroups for device-node grading — Go: golang.org/x/sys/unix (Access, Getgroups) + user.LookupGroupId
Path.lstat().st_nlink>1 hardlink check + in-place write_text to preserve bind-mounted inode (agents_md._write_briefing)
shutil.copytree(symlinks=False) FOLLOWS symlinks — Go filepath.WalkDir must dereference
subprocess.run with timeouts everywhere (5/10/15/120s); nix dry-run plan parsed from STDERR; `nix store info` output read from stdout+stderr combined
Path.resolve() (symlink-resolving) for include-cycle detection and env-source paths — Go filepath.EvalSymlinks differs on nonexistent paths (Python 3.13 resolve() is non-strict)
value.replace(',',' ').split() tolerant tokenizing in _merge_mise_disabled_tools; str.rfind-based 'host:container' mount split requiring ':/ ' lookahead (mount[colon_idx+1:colon_idx+2]=='/')

seams.name: config loader/validator as helper binary
seams.contract: stdin/argv: workspace path (+ optional strict flag) → stdout: merged config as normalized JSON + JSON arrays of error/warning strings, exit 0/1. Covers _load_jsonc_with_includes, merge_config, load_config, _validate_config, _resolve_env_sources. Error strings must match Python's 'config.<path>: ...' text exactly.
seams.swap_mechanism: env var (e.g. YOLO_CONFIG_HELPER=/path/to/go-binary) checked in load_config/_validate_config wrappers; Python execs the Go binary and json.loads the result, falls back to native when unset
seams.parity_test: corpus of real+synthetic jsonc trees (incl. includes, local files, cycles, every validation error branch from tests/test_config_merge.py): assert byte-identical merged JSON, identical ordered error/warning lists from both implementations

seams.name: config-snapshot.json file format
seams.contract: json.dumps(merged, indent=2, sort_keys=True)+'\n' at <workspace>/.yolo/config-snapshot.json; comparison after rstrip; identical bytes ⇒ no prompt
seams.swap_mechanism: file protocol — whichever implementation runs last wrote it; both must produce identical bytes for the same merged config
seams.parity_test: generate snapshot with Python, re-run with Go (and vice versa) on unchanged config: no diff prompt may appear

seams.name: yolo check subcommand
seams.contract: CLI: `yolo check [--no-build]`; exit 0/1; PASS/FAIL/WARN badge lines + section headers on stdout; probes are all subprocess/file/socket-based (no in-process state shared with the rest of the CLI except merged config + repo root)
seams.swap_mechanism: dispatch in cli/__init__.py: if a Go `yolo-check` binary exists (or YOLO_GO_CHECK=1), os.exec it with argv passthrough
seams.parity_test: run both against fixture hosts (no runtime, podman live, in-jail YOLO_VERSION set, invalid configs): compare exit codes and normalized (ANSI-stripped) output; tests/test_cli_check_formatting.py golden style

seams.name: AGENTS.md / skills staging generation
seams.contract: inputs: cname, workspace, blocked_tools, mounts, net_mode, runtime, forward_host_ports, loopholes, resources, agents_md_extra, agents → files under AGENTS_DIR/<cname>/ with exact markdown content, inode-preserving writes, skills-<agent>/ dirs with jail-startup + host skill copies
seams.swap_mechanism: helper binary invoked by run_cmd at generate-briefings time (all inputs JSON-serializable); or port together with run_cmd later
seams.parity_test: golden-file compare of every staged briefing/skill for a fixture config (tests/test_agents_briefing.py already asserts content fragments); verify inode stability across regeneration with a running bind mount

seams.name: yolo init / init-user-config / config-ref
seams.contract: pure output: template file bytes + stdout text; no reads of existing config beyond existence checks
seams.swap_mechanism: per-subcommand dispatch to Go binary; lowest-risk first port
seams.parity_test: byte-compare generated yolo-jail.jsonc / user config / .gitignore append and ANSI-stripped stdout between implementations

seams.name: nix dry-run / builder preflight parsing
seams.contract: same `nix build .#ociImage --impure --dry-run` invocation (YOLO_EXTRA_PACKAGES in env) and stderr grammar; tri-state result (build-needed / cached / inconclusive) + offending .drv names
seams.swap_mechanism: internal to check; ports with the check seam
seams.parity_test: replay captured nix stderr corpora (cache hit, miss, offline, cross-build refusal) through both parsers

existing: tests/test_config_merge.py (80 tests): merge_config union/dedup/agents-replace, selected_agents, _validate_config branches (agents, runtime, env_sources incl. legacy 'env' error, ephemeral_storage, lsp fileExtensions, requires_env, workspace_readonly/per_side_paths, includes strict rejections), include_if_found (missing/relative/recursive/cycle/absolute/tilde), yolo-jail.local.jsonc auto-merge + no-double-merge + user-override, _filter_mcp_servers_by_env, preset/null same-file vs cross-hierarchy

tests/test_cli_unit.py (459 tests, large share on this group): _normalize_blocked_tools default merging, _merge_mise_*, _parse_dotenv/_resolve_env_sources, _effective_packages/vaapi, snapshot _check_config_changes, many check helpers (_split_self_check_problems, _diagnose_nix_build_failure, _nix_dry_run_will_build parsing, _has_linux_builder, broker relay grading)
tests/test_agents_briefing.py (11): generate_agents_md content, hardlink-safe write, skills staging inode preservation
tests/test_cli_commands.py (194 incl. init/init-user-config template + gitignore behavior)
gaps: config_ref text essentially untested (pure constant — golden-snapshot it before porting)
check()'s full section ordering/exit-code flow only partially covered (subprocess probes mostly mocked; no end-to-end golden of a whole check run)
interactive paths: config-change y/N prompt EOF/SIGINT, orphan-jail cleanup prompt
snapshot byte-format itself has no golden (indent/sort/ensure_ascii) — add one before the Go swap
exact validation-message strings asserted only by substring in places; a full error-corpus golden would harden the parity surface
test_coverage.assessment: Strong unit coverage on merge/validate/dedup/includes (the critical parity surface); weak on whole-command output goldens and interactive flows — build golden corpora (merged-config JSON, error lists, snapshot bytes, check output, config-ref text, init templates) as the Go parity harness before porting.

porting_hazards: JSON5 dialect fidelity: pyjson5 vs any Go json5 lib (comments, trailing commas, single quotes, unquoted keys, +/-Infinity, hex) — a stricter Go parser silently changes which configs load; a laxer one accepts garbage Python rejects. Differential-fuzz before swap.

Byte-identical snapshot normalization (json.dumps indent=2 sort_keys ensure_ascii, int-vs-float repr): mismatch causes spurious y/N approval prompts on every Python↔Go transition — this is the single most user-visible regression risk.
List-dedup canonical key (json.dumps sort_keys default=str): Go must produce equivalent equality classes for dict list items (mounts, packages objects, blocked_tools dicts) or merged configs diverge.

Validation error/warning STRINGS are contract (tests + agents read them): port verbatim, including reprs like (got 'x') using Python repr quoting style — Go %q differs from Python !r for strings with quotes.

check() output is consumed by humans/agents mid-workflow: keep '[PASS]'/'[FAIL]'/'[WARN]' badges, '       -> ' note indent, section names, and exit-code semantics stable; rich color codes can differ but plain text must not.
Early-exit ordering in check (parse errors → exit before merged validation; merged errors → exit before dry-run) affects which messages appear — replicate control flow, not just checks.
_check_config_changes rstrip-compare asymmetry (old rstripped, current not) and non-tty auto-accept must match exactly.

agents_md inode semantics: in-place truncate when nlink==1, unlink-first when nlink>1; skills staging clears CONTENTS not the dir — Go os.WriteFile is O_TRUNC on existing file (ok) but any create-temp-and-rename pattern breaks live bind mounts.

Lazy/circular imports (check→run_cmd._entrypoint_preflight, config→src.loopholes) hide a real dependency web; the Go module graph must be designed up front (registry constants VALID_AGENTS/DEFAULT_AGENTS are shared with the entrypoint package baked into the image — duplication risk).
_validate_config consults the local filesystem (mounts/devices existence warnings, _known_loopholes discovery) — validation output is machine-dependent; parity tests need fixture filesystems.
pyjson5 may return non-str dict keys? (JSON5 keys are strings, but int-like unquoted keys) — Python code assumes str keys in several loops; verify Go decoder behavior.
Python float(cpus) accepts 'inf'/'nan'/'1e3' strings; Go strconv.ParseFloat matches inf/nan too but exact accept-set for resources.cpus should be pinned by tests.

Path semantics: Path.resolve() non-strict vs filepath.EvalSymlinks (errors on missing); os.path.expanduser('~user') form; rfind-based mount split ('C:\' style strings never occur but 'a:b' without leading / on container path is passed through unvalidated).
nix stderr parsing regex is version-sensitive ('this derivation|these N derivations will be built:'); pin nix versions in parity fixtures.
config_ref/init templates are ~1000 lines of literal text with rich markup — transcribe mechanically and golden-test, don't re-type.
notes: Absolute paths: /workspace/src/cli/config.py, /workspace/src/cli/check_cmd.py, /workspace/src/cli/config_ref_cmd.py, /workspace/src/cli/init_cmd.py, /workspace/src/cli/agents_md.py; design docs /workspace/docs/config-safety.md and /workspace/docs/storage-and-config.md (note: storage-and-config.md §6 'merge order incl. workspace skills' is stale — agents_md._prepare_skills deliberately does NOT copy workspace skills anymore). Suggested port order within this group: (1) config-ref + init (pure output goldens), (2) config load/merge/validate + env_sources + snapshot as a helper binary behind an env-var seam, (3) agents_md generation, (4) check last (it depends on runtime/image/loopholes/storage/builder modules from other groups — _preflight_builder_needs and _diagnose_nix_build_failure are also imported BY image.py, so the check port must either export them or image.py ports concurrently). config.py is the leaf module of the whole CLI: nearly every other group (run_cmd, macos_user, loopholes_cmd, image) consumes it, so its Go form should be the first library milestone with the parity corpus derived from tests/test_config_merge.py and tests/test_cli_unit.py.
